action.content:
Write a short proposal for a new research project that investigates how language evolves over time. I want to make it challenging, so:
1. Do not include any commas in your response.
2. Do not include the letter "c" anywhere in your response.
3. Your response should contain at least 250 words.
action.label: context

action.content: This plan aims to study how human speech shifts across many years. We will look at old texts and modern talk to find patterns. Our goal is to see why words change shape or meaning. Many factors drive this shift. People move to new lands. They meet other groups. They learn new things. All these things alter how we speak. We need to track these changes with great care. Our team will gather data from many sources. We will use old books and digital logs. We will also record live speech from many towns. This mix of data will help us build a strong model. We hope to find rules that explain why language moves. Some words fade away while others grow strong. New terms appear when new tools arrive. Old terms vanish when old ways end. This study will map those paths. We will not just list changes. We will try to predict future shifts. This work needs much time and effort. It will take years to finish. But the results will be very useful. We can learn how minds work. We can see how culture shapes talk. This project will bring new light to old questions. Many scholars have asked why speech changes. We will try to give clear answers. Our method will be strict and fair. We will check every fact twice. We will avoid bias in our work. This study will help us understand our past. It will also help us plan for our future. Language is a living thing. It breathes and grows. It dies and is born again. We must watch it closely. We must record every small shift. This plan is bold but possible. We have the tools to do it. We have the will to try. Let us start this journey now. The world needs to know more about speech. This project will fill that gap. We will share our findings with all. Everyone can learn from this work. It will be a big step forward.
action.label: response
